title: Paneflow configuration
description: Schema for paneflow.json. Lives at ~/.config/paneflow/paneflow.json on Linux, ~/Library/Application Support/paneflow/paneflow.json on macOS, and %APPDATA%\paneflow\paneflow.json on Windows. The runtime is lenient: unknown top-level keys are ignored, so the schema's `additionalProperties: false` is an editor-side aid, not a runtime constraint.

$schema.description: Pointer to this schema. Used by editors (VS Code, Zed, JetBrains) for autocomplete and validation. Ignored at runtime.

$schemaVersion.description: Schema version pin. Paneflow logs a warning when the value is unknown but never refuses to load the file.

default_shell.description: Shell binary path. When null or omitted, falls back to $SHELL, then /bin/sh on Unix, then %COMSPEC% on Windows.

font_family.description: Font family. Accepts the special aliases '.PaneflowMono' (bundled Lilex) and '.PaneflowSans' (bundled IBM Plex Sans), the concrete embedded names ('Lilex', 'IBM Plex Sans'), or any installed system monospace family. Unknown families fall back to Lilex with a warning.

window_decorations.description: Window decoration mode. 'client' = client-side decorations (custom title bar). 'server' = server-side decorations (compositor-drawn). Read once at startup; changes require a restart.

option_as_meta.description: Treat Alt/Option as Meta and send an ESC prefix. Default true on Linux and Windows. On macOS, set to false if you rely on Option to produce Unicode characters.

shortcuts.description: Custom keybindings overriding the defaults. Format: { 'ctrl+t': 'new_tab' }. The full list of action names is defined in src-app/src/app/actions.rs.

ligatures.description: Render programming-font ligatures (FiraCode '=>', '!=', JetBrains Mono, Cascadia Code). Hot-reloaded.

commands.description: cmux-compatible command and workspace definitions, rendered in the workspace command palette.

claude_code_bypass_permissions.description: When true, the built-in 'Claude Code' tab-bar button launches the CLI with --permission-mode bypassPermissions, disabling all permission prompts. Per Anthropic's docs this mode offers no protection against prompt injection — opt in only on machines where that risk is acceptable.

claude_code_button_visible.description: Show the built-in 'Claude Code' command button in the tab bar.

codex_button_visible.description: Show the built-in 'Codex' command button in the tab bar.

opencode_button_visible.description: Show the built-in 'Opencode' command button in the tab bar.

pi_button_visible.description: Show the built-in 'Pi' command button in the tab bar.

hermes_agent_button_visible.description: Show the built-in 'Hermes Agent' command button in the tab bar.

telemetry.description: Opt-in desktop telemetry consent. The first-run consent modal only appears when 'enabled' is unanswered.

enabled.description: Tri-state consent: null = unanswered, true = opted in, false = opted out. The PANEFLOW_NO_TELEMETRY=1 environment variable overrides this unconditionally.

commandDefinition.description: Either a workspace definition (set 'workspace') or a shell command (set 'command'). The two fields are mutually exclusive.

description.description: Human-readable description shown in the command palette.

command.description: Shell command string. Mutually exclusive with 'workspace'.

cwd.description: Default working directory for the workspace.

color.description: Workspace accent color as a 6-digit lowercase hex string (no leading #).

ratio.description: Legacy single ratio for binary (2-child) layouts. Ignored when 'ratios' is present.

ratios.description: Per-child ratios for N-ary layouts. When present, must match children length and sum to ~1.0.

surface_type.description: Surface kind. Currently only 'terminal' is rendered.

name.description: Display name for the surface tab.

command.description: Shell command to run on surface creation.

cwd.description: Working directory override (relative paths resolve against the workspace cwd).

focus.description: When true, this surface receives initial focus.
